token_family_docs.$description: Developer-facing catalogue of the .ob-* CSS classes that activate each mode value, plus, for the viewport mode, the from/to range boundaries. The application adds these classes to <body> or a container to switch the active mode; tokens scoped to that mode then resolve.

name.$description: Developer-facing class. The application adds it to <body> to activate the xs viewport mode, overriding the xl base. Scopes which viewport-dependent token values resolve.

from.$description: xs range start, inclusive — the xs breakpoint.

to.$description: xs range end, inclusive — one pixel below the sm breakpoint, so adjacent ranges do not overlap.

name.$description: Developer-facing class. The application adds it to <body> to activate the sm viewport mode, overriding the xl base. Scopes which viewport-dependent token values resolve.

from.$description: sm range start, inclusive — the sm breakpoint.

to.$description: sm range end, inclusive — one pixel below the md breakpoint, so adjacent ranges do not overlap.

name.$description: Developer-facing class. The application adds it to <body> to activate the md viewport mode, overriding the xl base. Scopes which viewport-dependent token values resolve.

from.$description: md range start, inclusive — the md breakpoint.

to.$description: md range end, inclusive — one pixel below the lg breakpoint, so adjacent ranges do not overlap.

name.$description: Developer-facing class. The application adds it to <body> to activate the lg viewport mode, overriding the xl base. Scopes which viewport-dependent token values resolve.

from.$description: lg range start, inclusive — the lg breakpoint.

to.$description: lg range end, inclusive — one pixel below the xl breakpoint, so adjacent ranges do not overlap.

name.$description: Developer-facing. xl is the base viewport: no class is applied to <body>. It is the first-paint default, rendered before any viewport class is set; other viewports override it via their .ob-viewport-* class.

from.$description: xl range start, inclusive — the xl breakpoint.

to.$description: xl range end, inclusive — one pixel below the 2xl breakpoint, so adjacent ranges do not overlap.

name.$description: Developer-facing class. The application adds it to <body> to activate the 2xl viewport mode, overriding the xl base. Scopes which viewport-dependent token values resolve.

from.$description: 2xl range start, inclusive — the 2xl breakpoint. Open-ended, no upper boundary.
